target.title: fix(heartbeat): avoid loading response tool via search
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex heartbeat collaboration instructions told the model to call `tool_search heartbeat_respond` when `heartbeat_respond` was not already exposed, which can surface a noisy tool failure during otherwise quiet heartbeat wakes.
- Solution: Prefer `heartbeat_respond` only when it is already available; otherwise end with a concise final heartbeat summary instead of trying to load the response tool through `tool_search`.
- What changed: The Codex app-server heartbeat collaboration instruction text now explicitly says not to call `tool_search` just to load `heartbeat_respond`, and the prompt snapshot/test expectations were updated.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not remove or rename `heartbeat_respond`, does not change heartbeat scheduling, and does not change notification routing or heartbeat response parsing.

AI-assisted PR: yes, authored with Codex under human direction.

## Motivation

- Heartbeats should be low-noise automation. If the structured response tool is already present, it is the best reporting path. If the tool is not present in a turn, asking Codex to search for it can create visible `tool_search heartbeat_respond` failures that are more disruptive than a plain final heartbeat summary.
- This keeps the structured path for runtimes that expose it while giving runtimes without that tool a clean fallback.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #84975
- Related #85416
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Heartbeat turns should not attempt to load `heartbeat_respond` through `tool_search` when the response tool is not already available.
- Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw Gateway LaunchAgent, installed package `OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (3c01d4a)` with the equivalent runtime prompt patch applied; local source checkout on current `origin/main` for tests.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Patched the installed runtime instruction, restarted `openclaw gateway`, and verified the gateway was healthy.
  - Enqueued a safe no-notify system event with `openclaw system event --mode now --expect-final --json --timeout 60000 --session-key agent:monitor:heartbeat-smoke --text 'Codex heartbeat smoke test. Do not notify the user. Finish with HEARTBEAT_SMOKE_OK.'`.
  - Checked the last heartbeat status with `openclaw system heartbeat last --json`.
  - In this source checkout: `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "uses turn-scoped collaboration instructions for heartbeat Codex turns"`.
  - In this source checkout: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts`.
  - In this source checkout: `pnpm check:changed --base origin/main` and `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
$ openclaw gateway status
Runtime: running (pid 77479)
Connectivity probe: ok
Capability: admin-capable

$ openclaw system event --mode now --expect-final --json --timeout 60000 --session-key agent:monitor:heartbeat-smoke --text 'Codex heartbeat smoke test. Do not notify the user. Finish with HEARTBEAT_SMOKE_OK.'
{
  "ok": true
}

$ openclaw system heartbeat last --json
{
  "status": "ok-token",
  "reason": "interval",
  "preview": "HEARTBEAT_OK",
  "durationMs": 44404,
  "channel": "discord",
  "silent": true,
  "indicatorType": "ok"
}
```

```text
$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "uses turn-scoped collaboration instructions for heartbeat Codex turns"
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 213 skipped (214)
```

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  10 passed (10)

$ pnpm check:changed --base origin/main
[check:changed] lanes=extensions, extensionTests, docs
[check:changed] typecheck extensions
[check:changed] typecheck extension tests
[check:changed] lint extensions
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
exit 0

$ git diff --check
exit 0
```

- Observed result after fix: The generated heartbeat collaboration instructions contain the new fallback wording and no longer contain `search for heartbeat_respond, load it, then call it`. The local gateway remained healthy and heartbeat status stayed silent/ok.
- What was not tested: I did not force a live heartbeat turn where `heartbeat_respond` is absent from the runtime but `tool_search` is present, because that requires manipulating the app-server dynamic tool surface in a live install. I also did not run the full repository test suite.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): A real local Discord heartbeat surfaced `tool_search heartbeat_respond (agent) failed` before this prompt change, matching the old instruction that explicitly asked Codex to search for and load the response tool.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Codex heartbeat collaboration prompt assumed `tool_search` was a safe way to recover the structured `heartbeat_respond` tool. In runtimes where the response tool is unavailable or not searchable for that turn, this instruction turns a missing optional reporting tool into a visible tool error.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The existing test asserted that the old `tool_search` recovery instruction was present, so prompt coverage preserved the noisy fallback instead of the intended low-noise behavior.
- Contributing context (if known): Heartbeats are often silent background wakes, so even harmless tool lookup failures are user-visible noise when they are posted back into chat surfaces.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` and `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/telegram-heartbeat-codex-tool.md`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Heartbeat collaboration instructions tell Codex not to call `tool_search` just to load `heartbeat_respond`, while still including the structured response-tool guidance when available.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug is prompt-contract text emitted before the model turn starts. The collaboration-mode unit test and committed prompt snapshot catch the exact regression without requiring a live model turn.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): The collaboration-mode test already covered heartbeat instructions; this PR flips the assertion to the corrected fallback wording and adds a negative assertion for the old search/load instruction.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Heartbeat wakes should stop producing visible `tool_search heartbeat_respond` lookup failures when the structured heartbeat response tool is absent. When `heartbeat_respond` is available, the prompt still tells Codex to use it.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
heartbeat turn -> heartbeat_respond missing -> tool_search heartbeat_respond -> possible visible tool error

After:
heartbeat turn -> heartbeat_respond missing -> concise final heartbeat summary -> no response-tool lookup
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: This narrows tool-use guidance by removing an automatic `tool_search` lookup path for an optional heartbeat reporting tool. It does not remove any callable tool or grant access to new tools.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw Gateway LaunchAgent for real smoke; local source worktree with pnpm workspace for tests
- Model/provider: Codex app-server prompt construction path
- Integration/channel (if any): Heartbeat/system event path; local heartbeat status was silent Discord heartbeat state
- Relevant config (redacted): default local gateway with heartbeats enabled

### Steps

1. Start a Codex heartbeat turn with collaboration-mode heartbeat instructions.
2. Inspect the emitted developer instructions.
3. Run a heartbeat/system event smoke in a patched local gateway.

### Expected

- The prompt says to use `heartbeat_respond` if already available.
- The prompt says not to call `tool_search` just to load `heartbeat_respond`.
- Silent heartbeat status remains ok without visible response-tool lookup failures.

### Actual

- Before this PR, the prompt instructed Codex to search for and load `heartbeat_respond` through `tool_search`.
- After this PR, that search/load instruction is absent and replaced with a concise final-summary fallback.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - Targeted heartbeat collaboration-mode test passed.
  - Prompt snapshot test passed.
  - `pnpm check:changed --base origin/main` passed.
  - `git diff --check` passed.
  - Patched local gateway remained healthy; heartbeat last status was `ok-token` and `silent: true`.
- Edge cases checked:
  - The prompt still references `heartbeat_respond` as the preferred structured path when available.
  - The old `search for heartbeat_respond, load it, then call it` instruction is explicitly absent in the unit test.
  - Normal/user turns still do not receive heartbeat collaboration instructions.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full repository test suite.
  - Live channel heartbeat with a deliberately missing `heartbeat_respond` tool and present `tool_search` tool.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A heartbeat runtime that previously depended on searchable `heartbeat_respond` might now return a final summary instead of a structured response.
  - Mitigation: This only affects turns where the structured tool is not already exposed. Runtimes that expose `heartbeat_respond` still receive guidance to use it.
- Risk: Plain final summaries may carry less structured heartbeat metadata than `heartbeat_respond`.
  - Mitigation: The fallback applies only when the structured tool is unavailable; it avoids turning optional metadata capture into a visible tool error.
target.author: bryanpearson
